title: I Bought These 2 Stocks Again During the Crash. Here's Why

buy_recommendations.reason: The creator describes this as their highest conviction name and believes it will become a gigantic company in the future. The investment thesis is based on the premise that there will not be one AI model that wins everything; instead, there will be multiple AI models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, open source models, etc.) competing. As more AI models and companies enter the ecosystem, demand for compute tokens increases. Nebeus benefits from this token generation regardless of which model or company is winning at any given time. The creator notes that cheaper tokens and more efficient models actually expand the total addressable market for AI compute, driving ecosystem growth and increasing overall demand for compute—benefiting Nebeus. The creator expects to be very surprised by Nebeus's results, guidance, and management comments, and believes the current market dip presents a buying opportunity.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: During market dips; creator added 10 shares at $181 during the crash
buy_recommendations.target_price: The creator believes Nebeus will be a $100+ billion company
buy_recommendations.cautions: None explicitly stated, though the creator acknowledges that understanding this thesis is important and 'may not be for everyone'

buy_recommendations.reason: The creator has been accumulating shares at lower prices after taking significant profits earlier (sold 45 shares at $114 and 100 shares near $87). The strategy is to buy back shares at lower valuations. The creator stated earlier they would continue to accumulate below $80, and since the stock fell below that target into the low $70s, this presented a buying opportunity. The creator added 30 shares at $68.36 during the crash.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: Under $80, ideally in the low $70s; creator bought at $68.36
buy_recommendations.cautions: The creator notes that Rocket Lab should never have been at $150 per share this early in its development. While the creator likes the company, they acknowledge it was overvalued at those heights.

buy_recommendations.reason: The creator still likes Alphabet and believes it can become the biggest and most valuable company on the planet. The investment case is based on: (1) Google Cloud revenue accelerating with improving margins; (2) operating margin of 32.7% over the last 12 months is excellent; (3) the backlog nearly doubled to $467.6 billion last quarter, with potential to reach $500-600 billion or higher this quarter, which the creator finds insane and insists they trust Google more than competitors like Oracle; (4) strong free cash flow generation despite current capex investments; (5) expected revenue growth of 21% year-over-year this fiscal year, then 18.7%, then 18% in fiscal 2028 to reach ~$682.8 billion (creator expects it could be closer to $700 billion); (6) the creator expects record-breaking quarterly results. The company is investing heavily due to insane demand for cloud compute, which is very good for the AI cycle.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: During the current market dip; creator did not mention adding during crash but expressed continued conviction
buy_recommendations.cautions: The creator emphasizes the need to wait and see what the capex update will be. Free cash flow margin has been coming down, which investors should monitor. The forward P/E of 27.2x is quite high, though this is partly due to heavy capex investments in data centers and TPUs.

buy_recommendations.reason: The creator picked Intel in a stock draft, calling it a good opportunity. The thesis is that a good Intel could be worth a trillion dollars. Specific positives cited include: (1) Intel has had a tremendous revival, up 306% over the past 12 months; (2) the CPU story is still going very well; (3) Intel Foundry Services (18A and 14A processes) represent important upcoming developments; (4) data center business remains strong; (5) momentum is building; (6) the company has been beating expectations more recently. The creator notes the company is moving in the right direction.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: During the current 33% drawdown from June 22nd highs; no specific entry point given
buy_recommendations.target_price: Creator suggests a good Intel could be worth $1 trillion, though acknowledges a $500 billion valuation may also be far-fetched currently
buy_recommendations.cautions: The creator notes that a lot of things need to go Intel's way for the high valuation to be justified, and results haven't yet justified even a $500 billion valuation. The creator does not feel the need to own Intel personally, suggesting it may be more speculative. Currently trading at 33% drawdown, so still showing weakness.

buy_recommendations.reason: The creator views Uber's acquisition of Delivery Hero as a very good strategic move that keeps the company super competitive while making it even stronger. The investment case is based on Uber having tremendous leadership that 'does all the right moves.' The creator has added significantly to Uber.
buy_recommendations.cautions: The creator stated they do not plan to add more right now, as they want to prioritize increasing exposure to other positions like Nebeus and Rocket Lab.

buy_recommendations.reason: The creator mentions wanting to increase exposure to Axon but does not provide detailed reasoning in this video segment.
The creator mentions wanting to increase Reddit position but does not provide detailed reasoning in this video segment.
The creator mentions potentially buying more SoFi if it comes down in price but does not provide detailed investment reasoning.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: If the stock price comes down
buy_recommendations.cautions: The creator is monitoring this as a potential addition but is not currently committed to adding to the position.

stocks_to_avoid.reason: The creator uses Navitas as an example of a 'trash company' that has gone up significantly and is now down 60% from highs. The creator argues that Navitas should have never been valued so highly in the first place. The hype was driven by Nvidia's involvement and a blog post, but the creator states that right now Navitas has nothing to show on paper. While the stock will likely go up when everything else goes up, the creator questions whether investors should own such companies given the risk of flash crashes and lack of real business fundamentals to justify billions in valuation.
stocks_to_avoid.risk: Down 60% from highs; creator suggests it will experience similar flash crashes in the future

stocks_to_avoid.company_name: Axiom Space (or similar space company referenced as ASND/Asnd in context)
stocks_to_avoid.reason: The creator argues that space companies with inflated valuations like Asnd should never have commanded such high valuations this early in their development. The example given is a company that was valued at $50 billion+ at some point. The creator criticizes the flawed logic of '60 partners and three billion subscribers potential'—noting that three billion subscriber potential means nothing if nobody will pay extra for the service. In the context of satellite internet, the creator points out that most urban dwellers don't need satellite connectivity, making the addressable market much smaller than implied by such projections. Even today, according to the creator, such valuations 'just don't make any sense.'
stocks_to_avoid.risk: Extreme overvaluation relative to addressable market; likely to experience significant crashes

key_warnings: Market volatility expected to continue due to: (1) ongoing earnings season with major tech companies reporting (Alphabet, Tesla, ServiceNow, IBM, Intel, Nokia); (2) geopolitical issues that will likely resurface post-World Cup; (3) AI sector experiencing significant volatility as market reassesses valuations and competitive dynamics.

Fed rate probability expectations are extremely volatile and change weekly. Currently 26.2% probability of zero rate changes in 2026 (up from 12.6% one week ago and 11.5% one month ago), but this could swing back if inflation or gas prices increase. Investors should not rely on Fed probability shifts as investment timing signals.

Market misunderstanding of AI ecosystem dynamics. The creator warns against the notion that the AI cycle is ending because of new competitive models like Qwen/K3 from Qwen. In reality, cheaper and more efficient models expand the total addressable market for compute, driving MORE total demand for GPU compute and tokens—not less. This is being misunderstood by the market.

Distinction between quality and trash companies during downturns. The creator emphasizes that not all beaten-down stocks represent buying opportunities. Companies like Navitas and Asnd that were overvalued and lack real business fundamentals should be avoided, even after significant declines. Do real research and distinguish between good companies experiencing temporary dips versus fundamentally flawed businesses that deserve to be repriced lower.

Forward P/E multiples for major tech companies remain elevated (e.g., Alphabet at 27.2x), justified only if revenue growth and free cash flow generation continue to meet or exceed expectations. Capex spending on AI infrastructure is currently depressing free cash flow metrics, which investors must monitor closely in upcoming earnings.
